mechanism_narrative: High and persistently rising inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes real corporate earnings and increases the likelihood of aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank. This leads to higher discount rates and reduced investor confidence, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 278.919 in Nov 2021, indicating elevated inflation.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL shows positive month-over-month growth, or the Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a critical threshold indicates an overheating labor market. This typically precedes wage inflation and potential central bank hawkishness, negatively impacting corporate profit margins and equity valuations over the medium term.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 4.0% and stays below for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 4.1% in Nov 2021, near historical lows.
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods following the trigger, or the average return in triggered periods is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) combined with an exceptionally low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals strong consumer demand supported by highly accommodative monetary conditions. This environment is highly favorable for corporate earnings growth and equity prices, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 0.08% in Nov 2021, indicating very low rates.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where both conditions are met, or the statistical interaction term between RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS is not significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, a sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals growing consumer confidence and investment in real estate. This has a broad positive multiplier effect on the economy and corporate earnings, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 1579.0 (thousands of units) in Oct 2021.
failure_condition: The signal fails to produce positive returns during identified recovery periods, or the correlation between HOUST growth and SPX returns is negative during such regimes over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy and expanding labor market. This supports robust consumer spending and corporate revenues, which are fundamental drivers of positive S&P 500 forward returns over short horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 149230.0 (thousands of persons) in Nov 2021.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth, or the Rank IC is consistently below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and persistent decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall economic activity. This negatively impacts corporate earnings and leads to lower S&P 500 forward returns as investor sentiment deteriorates.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods following the trigger, or the average return in triggered periods is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) creates significant pressure on the central bank to tighten monetary policy. This leads to higher interest rates (FEDFUNDS) and a negative impact on equity valuations due to increased discount rates and heightened recession fears.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL 12-month growth was significantly above 5% in Nov 2021.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where both conditions are met, or the interaction term's coefficient between inflation and unemployment is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic expansion, robust GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a healthy and expanding economy. This translates directly into higher corporate profits and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence and earnings expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 3% for 2 consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 revised value was 21617.828 in Q3 2021.
failure_condition: The signal fails to produce positive returns during identified strong expansion periods, or the correlation between GDPC1 growth and SPX returns is negative during such regimes over any 3-year rolling window.
